name: ValidateCommand
purpose: Check that the command is one of the four valid commands
behavior: If command is not in ['send', 'history', 'create_channel', 'join'], return error result

semantic_outputs.name: validation_result
semantic_outputs.description: Either success with command or error result

postconditions: If invalid, parent returns error immediately

inputs.name: command
inputs.description: The chat command string to validate

outputs.name: validation_result
outputs.description: Result dict with success (bool) and message (str). If invalid, success=False and message describes error.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=False with error message for invalid commands

name: SendMessage
purpose: Handle 'send' command: validate membership, store message, update last_seen, notify members
behavior: Check user is member of channel via channels store, if not return error. Store message in messages store with timestamp. Update user's last_seen in users store. Return success with message data.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel data store for membership check

semantic_outputs.name: send_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success, data, message

guarantees: Only sends if user is member

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel data store for membership check

outputs.name: send_result
outputs.description: Result dict with success (bool), data (dict with message_id, content, timestamp, channel_id, user_id), message (str)

signature: def SendMessage(message_data: dict, channels: Database, messages: Database, users: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Write new message to messages store

data_operations.description: Check if user is member of channel

constraints.description: Must verify channel membership before writing message
Must generate and attach timestamp to message

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is not channel member
Message stored with correct channel_id, user_id, content, timestamp
User's last_seen updated after successful send

name: GetHistory
purpose: Handle 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from channel sorted by timestamp descending
behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return as list

semantic_outputs.name: history_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success and list of messages

outputs.name: history_result
outputs.description: Result dict with success (bool), data (list of message dicts with content, timestamp, channel_id, user_id), message (str)

signature: def GetHistory(message_data: dict, messages: Database) -> dict

constraints.description: Must sort messages by timestamp descending

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of messages for given channel_id
List is sorted by timestamp descending

name: CreateChannel
purpose: Handle 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set creator as first member
behavior: Check if channel name already exists in channels store, if so return error. Generate new channel_id, store channel with name, members=[user_id], created_by=user_id. Return success with channel data.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel store for reading and writing

semantic_outputs.name: create_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success and new channel data

postconditions: New channel exists in channels store

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel store for reading and writing

outputs.name: create_result
outputs.description: Result dict with success (bool), data (dict with channel_id, channel_name, members, created_by), message (str)

signature: def CreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read existing channels for uniqueness check, write new channel

data_operations.description: Check if channel name already exists
Store new channel with creator as first member

constraints.description: Must ensure channel name is unique

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if channel name already exists
New channel stored with name, members=[creator], created_by=creator

name: JoinChannel
purpose: Handle 'join' command: add user to channel member list if not already a member
behavior: Check if user is already in channel's members list, if so return error. Add user_id to members list. Return success.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel store for reading and writing

semantic_outputs.name: join_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success and message

postconditions: User added to channel members if not already

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel store for reading and writing

outputs.name: join_result
outputs.description: Result dict with success (bool), data (dict with channel_id, members), message (str)

signature: def JoinChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read channel members for duplicate check, write updated members list

data_operations.description: Check if user is already a member
Add user to channel members list

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is already a member
User added to channel members list
